versions.prompt: Landscape photography, golden hour. A wide view of snow-dusted peaks at sunset with warm amber and burnt-orange light flooding the scene. Scattered evergreen trees in the foreground, a thin layer of mist in the valley. The light is soft and golden, hitting the snow with warmth, not coldness. No people, no buildings, no ski lifts — but it feels like a place someone calls home, not a postcard. Slightly faded film tones, natural grain. Color palette: warm amber (#D4915E), deep forest shadow (#1A2A1F), snow catching golden light (#F5E6D0). The mood is the end of a long day outside, looking back at the mountains from the trailhead. Shot on 35mm film, editorial quality like a Patagonia catalog or The Surfer's Journal.
versions.analysis: Strong golden hour warmth with snow-dusted peaks and evergreens. Amber light hits the snow convincingly. Film-like quality achieved. Feels like a real place, not a stock photo. Good Patagonia catalog energy.
versions.iterate_notes: Could use slightly more mist in the valley. The foreground trees could be more prominent for depth.

versions.prompt: Added prominent foreground trees and thick valley mist to v0 base prompt.
versions.analysis: Much better depth with big foreground trees and golden mist filling the valley. The layering creates a stronger sense of place.
versions.iterate_notes: Try deeper color saturation — push the amber/golden tones further toward Kodachrome warmth.

versions.prompt: Deep amber Kodachrome sunset with saturated warm tones, building on v1 composition.
versions.analysis: Gorgeous amber sunset with golden mist. Great silhouetted trees against the warm sky. Kodachrome saturation adds richness.
versions.iterate_notes: Explore warm-to-cool color contrast — last light hitting peaks with cooler shadows. Try off-center composition.

versions.prompt: Last light with warm-to-cool contrast, off-center composition with dramatic peaks.
versions.analysis: Dramatic Fitz Roy-like peaks with great warm/cool contrast. The off-center composition creates visual tension and energy.
versions.iterate_notes: Try an intimate, closer view — morning instead of sunset, looking through pine trees at a single peak. Backyard mountain feel.

versions.prompt: Intimate morning view through pine trees at a single peak, mist in valley, pine needles on ground.
versions.analysis: Nailed the intimate 'backyard mountain' feel. Morning mist drifting through, pine needles scattered on the ground. Feels personal, not epic — like your favorite local peak.
versions.iterate_notes: Could experiment with different times of day — early blue hour or mid-morning side light. Consider adding a subtle trail or path element.
